2605.06040.title: Novelty-based Tree-of-Thought Search for LLM Reasoning and Planning
2605.06040.paper_summary: Although advances such as chain-of-thought, tree-of-thought or reinforcement learning have improved the performance of LLMs in reasoning and planning tasks, they are still brittle and have not achieved human-level performance in many domains, and often suffer from high time and token costs. Inspired by the success of width-based search in planning, we explore how the concept of novelty can be transferred to language domains and how it can improve tree-of-thought reasoning. A tree of thoughts relies on building possible "paths" of consecutive ideas or thoughts. These are generated by repeatedly prompting an LLM. In our paper, a measurable concept of novelty is proposed that describes the uniqueness of a new node (thought) in comparison to nodes previously seen in the search tree. Novelty is estimated by prompting an LLM and making use of embedded general knowledge from pre-training. This metric can then be used to prune branches and reduce the scope of the search. Although this method introduces more prompts per state, the overall token cost can be reduced by pruning and reducing the overall tree size. This procedure is tested and compared using several benchmarks in language-based planning and general reasoning.
2605.06040.paper_summary_zh: 儘管像是思維鏈、思維樹或強化學習等進展已經改善了大型語言模型在推理和規劃任務中的表現，但它們仍然脆弱，並且在許多領域尚未達到人類水平的表現，且常常面臨高時間和標記成本。受到基於寬度搜索在規劃中成功的啟發，我們探索了新穎性概念如何轉移到語言領域，以及它如何改善思維樹推理。一棵思維樹依賴於構建連續想法或思維的可能“路徑”。這些是通過不斷提示大型語言模型生成的。在我們的論文中，提出了一個可測量的新穎性概念，用於描述新節點（想法）與搜索樹中先前見過的節點相比的獨特性。新穎性是通過提示大型語言模型並利用預訓練中的嵌入通用知識來估算的。然後可以使用這個指標來修剪分支並減少搜索範圍。儘管這種方法在每個狀態下引入了更多的提示，但通過修剪和減少整體樹的大小，總體的標記成本可以降低。這一程序使用幾個基準在基於語言的規劃和一般推理中進行測試和比較。
2605.06040.author: Leon Hamm et.al.
